phase: MANUAL-VERIFICATION Phase 0, Step 5 (claim extraction + mapping only)

claims.claim: Production health endpoint returns {"status":"ok","version":"0.2.0"}
claims.method: curl -sf https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/health; compare JSON body
claims.adjudicationNote: curl -sf .../api/health -> {"status":"ok","version":"0.2.0"} HTTP 200, this-run probe.

claims.claim: Backend services active on production: aether-api, aether-web, aether-worker, redis-server (systemd units)
claims.method: Per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md sanctioned method, confirm each unit is active (systemctl is-active); corroborate functionally via async job completion (worker+redis alive)
claims.adjudicationNote: systemctl is-active aether-api aether-web aether-worker redis-server -> all 4 active, this-run probe.

claims.claim: AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION is left permanently true in production (async generation is the default path)
claims.method: Trigger a tailor/cover-letter/pipeline run on prod UI; expect HTTP 202 {job_id,status:enqueued}, not a synchronous 200
claims.adjudicationNote: Every tailor/cover-letter/pipeline POST returned 202 {job_id,enqueued} across both screens' live runs; .env AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=true confirmed fresh (name+value only, not secret).

claims.claim: Repo is clean: local main only, origin refs/heads/main only, 0 open PRs, exactly 1 branch
claims.method: git branch -a && gh pr list --state open (repo check, not live UI)
claims.adjudicationNote: Literal claim ('exactly 1 branch') false for CURRENT mid-run state: 32+ local fix/* branches + ~30 worktree-agent-* branches, local main 78 commits ahead of origin/main, 21 uncommitted paths. 0-open-PRs sub-claim holds (gh pr list --state open = empty). Per known orchestrator context this is expected mid-run and will be re-confirmed at gate G-08 (final push/cleanup not yet done).

claims.claim: PUT /api/agents/providers/anthropic/credential (and the per-user PUT /api/agents/user/providers/anthropic/credential) auto-detect and accept two credential formats: a Console API key (sk-ant-api03-...) sent via x-api-key header, OR a Claude Code OAuth token (sk-ant-oat01-...) sent via Authorization: Bearer + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20 header
claims.method: Submit both credential formats via the agent-config credential UI/API on prod; inspect stored authMode and confirm the correct auth header is used on the subsequent live call
claims.adjudicationNote: Live PUT of both sk-ant-api03-... and sk-ant-oat01-... test values; server derived/stored correct authMode each time; reproduced on 2 screens.

claims.claim: A mismatched or unrecognized Anthropic credential prefix is rejected with HTTP 422 naming both accepted formats (sk-ant-api03-.../sk-ant-oat01-...)
claims.method: Submit a garbage-prefixed credential via the credential UI/API, expect 422 with a message naming both accepted formats
claims.adjudicationNote: Garbage-prefix credential -> live 422 naming both sk-ant-api/sk-ant-oat01- formats verbatim, reproduced on 2 screens.

claims.claim: The x-api-key header returns 401 when used with an oat01 (OAuth) token -- the auth header sent must match the credential type stored
endpoints: (live Anthropic API call via stored credential)
claims.method: With an oat01-mode credential configured, force/trace the outgoing header on a live agent call and confirm x-api-key would 401 (verify via billingAudit.authMode + header trace, not by deliberately misusing a real secret)
claims.adjudicationNote: No live oat01 credential available (operator token expired, see CLM-010/H-5) so adjudicated by source read: header selection branches strictly on server-derived auth_mode (x-api-key for api_key, Authorization Bearer+anthropic-beta for oauth_token, RuntimeError otherwise); authMode is server-derived from secret prefix, not client-settable.

claims.claim: An oauth_token-mode Anthropic credential is stored encrypted (never in the clear) and, on save, is atomically synced to the repo-root .env as CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (0600 permissions, value never logged)
claims.method: Per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md sanctioned access: after saving the credential, check .env file permissions are 0600 and that the DB-stored value is not plaintext -- inspect metadata/format only, never log the full secret (first 8 chars max)
claims.adjudicationNote: Source-verified: Fernet ciphertext stored in DB (never plaintext); .env sync via tempfile.mkstemp in same dir -> os.fchmod(0o600) BEFORE os.replace() atomic rename; no logger/print call anywhere in the write path references the raw token value.

claims.claim: Quota exhaustion never silently falls through to a different billing credential -- an explicit HTTP 429 is raised instead
claims.method: Exhaust or simulate quota exhaustion on a test account and confirm 429 is returned, not a silent billing-credential switch
claims.adjudicationNote: Analogous, earlier-firing subscription-paywall (402) gate confirmed explicit/honest, never a silent fallthrough. The literal quota-429 code path could not be reached live (requires a paid+exhausted account); source-confirmed only (_plan_quota_429, agents.py:966-990).

claims.claim: A live sk-ant-oat01- token round-tripped through a genuine Anthropic API call (HTTP 200), and a live tailor run recorded billingAudit.authMode=oauth_token
claims.method: Re-run the test-connection action for the configured oat01 credential and a tailor run; inspect billingAudit.authMode field on the resulting AgentRun
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-agents-004 (VERIFIED-CLOSED, reproduced twice in independent sessions): live 'Test connection' round-trip to api.anthropic.com returns HTTP 401 'OAuth access token has expired', not 200. CROSS-CUTTING-NOTES.md: 3 agents observed, ALL served via OpenRouter (deepseek/qwen), none via Anthropic. resume-studio: 4/4 live tailor runs show authMode:api_key/provider:openrouter, never oauth_token. BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN H-5 confirms token expiry is operator-held, not further fixable by this run. NOTE: agent-monitor's tester independently reported CONFIRMED with a billingAudit.authMode=oauth_token reading -- this conflicts with the above; resolved in favor of REFUTED because MV-agents-004 is a later, twice-reproduced, directly-mechanistic live 401 plus 3 independent corroborating sources (cross-cutting notes, resume-studio, BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN H-5) against 1 earlier single reading, and matches this run's own known-context finding per the task briefing.

claims.claim: The in-app interactive "Connect with Anthropic" OAuth-consent flow remains removed and unsupported (only paste-token credential entry exists)
claims.method: Inspect agent-monitor/agents/settings UI for absence of an interactive OAuth-consent button; curl GET/POST /api/agents/auth/anthropic/start expecting 404
claims.adjudicationNote: GET+POST /agents/auth/anthropic/start -> 404 both, no OAuth-consent UI button found on 3 separate screens; re-confirmed fresh by this auditor's own curl probe (404/404).

claims.claim: AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS (default aether.local) is an exact-domain allowlist: an aether.local settings email is accepted; a different domain sharing only the .local label (e.g. evil.local / sub.aether.local) is rejected with 422
claims.method: Attempt to save a settings email at aether.local (expect success/200) and at evil.local or sub.aether.local (expect 422)
claims.adjudicationNote: aether.local -> 200+persist; evil.local and sub.aether.local -> 422 identical message; verified twice.

claims.claim: PUT /api/workspaces/settings persists an email change (a prior defect where the UPDATE "User" statement silently omitted the email field, returning a false-positive HTTP 200, has been fixed) -- confirmed via direct production-DB read
claims.method: Change the settings email via UI/API, then GET the settings back (and/or a direct DB read per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md) to confirm the new value persisted
claims.adjudicationNote: PUT persists email change; GET + direct DB read both confirm new value; DB row shows updatedAt matching test window.

claims.claim: POST /api/agents/{tailor,cover-letters}/run and the composite pipeline endpoint return HTTP 202 {"job_id","status":"enqueued"} (async mode); caller polls GET /api/agents/jobs/{job_id} for terminal state
claims.method: Trigger a tailor/cover-letter/pipeline run via prod UI; confirm 202 + job_id in response; poll the jobs endpoint to a terminal state
claims.adjudicationNote: Exact 202 {job_id,status:enqueued} response shape observed; full enqueue->poll->terminal cycle directly observed on 5+ separate runs across both screens.

claims.claim: Quota is reserved atomically at enqueue time (not at execution) and refunded on enqueue failure, worker failure, or a stale-job watchdog trip
claims.method: Check quota/usage counters before and after triggering an async run, including a forced-failure scenario if reproducible; confirm refund on failure
claims.adjudicationNote: Source-confirmed mechanism (agents.py:966-990); not self-triggered live (paywall-blocked account + do-not-run-agents-wastefully discipline). Historical DB evidence of one failed-run refund is consistent but not fresh/self-produced.

claims.claim: The async worker runs under systemd unit aether-worker.service (ARQ task runner, Requires=redis-server.service), logging to /var/log/aether/worker.log
claims.method: Per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md (deployment authority) ONLY -- confirm the unit name/status and log path exactly as documented there; do not invent alternate paths if the runbook differs
claims.adjudicationNote: systemctl show aether-worker.service: ActiveState=active; unit file shows Requires=redis-server.service and StandardOutput/StandardError=append:/var/log/aether/worker.log exactly as claimed; tail of worker.log shows live activity at 00:15:00Z (cron:sweep_stale_jobs), i.e. actively running at probe time.

claims.claim: A 20-run production soak of async tailor/cover-letter generation showed 0 HTTP 503s, 0 fixture-fallback matches, 20/20 completed; a forced-failure run proved atomic quota refund
claims.method: Re-run a smaller soak (e.g. 5-10 generations) on prod and confirm 0 HTTP 503s / 0 fixture-fingerprint matches (full 20-run reproduction may be impractical -- partial soak is acceptable evidence)
claims.adjudicationNote: 0/4 fixture-fingerprint matches confirmed (holds). But 'zero HTTP 503s' does NOT hold in this run's window: cover-letter-studio observed a genuine 503 in-session, resume-studio's pipeline coverLetter step hit 2 genuine 503s. All were honest (no fixture substitution) -- MV-cover-letter-studio-005 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) confirms the failure messages are honest but leaked internal wording (fixed to be user-friendly). Atomic quota refund on failure INFERRED from cost fields, not independently forced-and-observed.

claims.claim: A scoped X-Aether-System-Run header (checked against AETHER_SYSTEM_RUN_SECRET) bypasses the subscription paywall ONLY for scout/fitScorer (deterministic, zero-LLM-token agents) -- never for tailor/coverLetter/pipeline, and never for an unpaid interactive user lacking the header
claims.method: As an unpaid test account: call scout/run with header+secret (expect success, bypassing paywall) and call tailor/run with header+secret (expect still-402, header does not exempt metered agents)
claims.adjudicationNote: Requires an unpaid test account PLUS the AETHER_SYSTEM_RUN_SECRET value simultaneously, neither available to a screen-tester nor to this auditor without generating a fresh account and exposing a live secret header. Source inspection confirms scout/fitScorer are exempt from the paywall while tailor is not, but this is not fresh live-triggered evidence.

claims.claim: The aether-discovery.timer cron fires unattended and completes end-to-end with the system-run header present, with zero 402s and zero exit-22 failures
claims.method: Per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md, inspect systemctl/journalctl status for aether-discovery.timer/.service for a recent successful unattended fire
claims.findingNote: NO existing finding in MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json (searched for discovery-cron/aether-discovery/discovery.timer/discovery.service -- zero hits). NEW FINDING NEEDED -- orchestrator must file.
claims.adjudicationNote: Timer fires on schedule (systemctl list-timers confirms last fire 2026-07-20T00:00:54Z, 12min before probe) -- that part holds. But the triggered aether-discovery.service has FAILED (exit-code 1) at the very first step (POST /auth/login -> 401) on every attempt for the full timestamped window of api.log (2026-07-18T23:00:26Z through 2026-07-20T00:00:54Z, ~25.5h, 0 successes; 123 total 401s from the cron's source IP across the full untimestamped+timestamped log). /var/log/aether/discovery.log itself has not been appended to since 2026-07-18T00:31:18Z. Likely root cause (not confirmed via any secret-exposing command): the discovery cron's default account (sarkar.vikram@gmail.com, per scripts/discovery_cron.sh) was wiped in the 2026-07-18 prod DB incident and per docs/delivery/INCIDENT-PROD-DB-WIPE-2026-07-18.md was expected to be re-created by the user re-signing-up, which had evidently not happened (or not with a matching password) as of this probe. The claim's 'zero 402s and zero exit-22 failures' is moot -- the failure mode is 401-at-login before the system-run-header/paywall logic is ever reached, which is arguably worse (total non-function, not merely an edge-limited residual).

claims.claim: 23 duplicate PDF/DOCX exports of canonical .md docs were removed from the repo; no .md content changed
claims.method: Repo file inventory check (git log / find) for duplicate PDF/DOCX exports of docs -- not a live-UI check
claims.adjudicationNote: git log --diff-filter=D across all history for *.pdf/*.docx finds exactly ONE removal commit (c07e06d, on main), whose --numstat shows only 5 files deleted (docs/research/*.pdf), not 23. 0 .md files touched in that commit (sub-claim CONFIRMED). Fresh scan of the current tree (excl. node_modules/worktrees) for any .md file with a same-basename .pdf/.docx sibling: zero matches today. Substance holds (dupes removed, .md untouched, 0 dupes now); the specific count '23' is not corroborated by git history.

claims.claim: New Phase-7 environment variables are present in the production .env: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS, AETHER_REDIS_URL, AETHER_REDIS_PASSWORD, AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION, AETHER_LLM_WORKER_BUDGET_SECONDS, AETHER_LLM_WORKER_COVER_BUDGET_SECONDS, AETHER_LLM_WORKER_PIPELINE_BUDGET_SECONDS, AETHER_SYSTEM_RUN_SECRET, AETHER_JOB_STALE_SECONDS
claims.method: Per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md sanctioned access, confirm presence of these env-var NAMES only in production .env (never log values)
claims.adjudicationNote: 9 of 10 named env vars present in repo-root .env (names checked only, values not logged except AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=true which is non-secret). AETHER_JOB_STALE_SECONDS is MISSING from .env; code has a safe default of 900s (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1082) so behavior is unaffected, but the literal claim of presence is false for that one var.

claims.claim: Phase-7 adversarial re-audit of 21 Phase-6 claims: 10 CONFIRMED, 8 PARTIALLY-TRUE, 2 raw-REFUTED (0 unresolved), 1 UNVERIFIABLE -- no Phase-6 claim was REFUTED-and-left-standing
claims.method: Document cross-check against PHASE7-CLAIM-LEDGER.md (a prior claim ledger, itself testimony) -- cannot be independently re-derived by driving the live app; treat prior ledger only as a pointer for what to re-test now, not as evidence
claims.adjudicationNote: Meta-claim about a PRIOR Phase-7 audit's own internal tally (10/8/2/1 breakdown of a 21-claim re-audit). Its own verification method self-disqualifies: 'cannot be independently re-derived by driving the live app; treat prior ledger only as a pointer.' No live production behavior can confirm or refute a historical audit's internal count. The individual Phase-6 claims that audit covered are separately, freshly re-adjudicated elsewhere in this ledger.

claims.claim: Four pre-seeded Phase-7 gap hypotheses were rejected on fresh evidence: FIXTURE-001 (0/60 fixture fingerprints in prod), SRC-001 (33 jobs/5 sources, floor exceeded), REPO-001 (already 1 branch/0 PRs), NONPROD-001 (0 suspicious matches; replay-guard present)
claims.method: Re-probe fixture fingerprints in live generation output; re-count live jobs/sources via GET /jobs; repo branch/PR check; grep for non-prod code markers
claims.adjudicationNote: FIXTURE-001 (0/60 fixture fingerprints) and SRC-001 (sourcing floor exceeded) re-confirmed fresh. REPO-001 (branch/PR state) and NONPROD-001 (non-prod code markers) are repo-level checks, UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI by a screen-tester -- see CLM-004/CLM-028 in this ledger for this auditor's own direct repo-level checks of those two.

claims.claim: All 27 core Phase-7 gates were VERIFIED-CLOSED including: 0 console errors across 20 routes (GATE-16), 0 same-origin 5xx (GATE-17), backend 676 pytest passed (GATE-19), frontend 297 vitest passed (GATE-20), Playwright E2E green (GATE-21)
claims.method: Playwright console/network sweep across the 20 documented routes on prod; re-run backend pytest / frontend vitest suites (suite-run for counts); manual click-through spot-check for E2E flows
claims.adjudicationNote: Suite-green sub-claim REFUTED both historically and currently: MV-system-002 found 3 credential-resolution tests red at the clean Phase-7 baseline (53f0e08), not 676/0. THIS-RUN'S OWN latest serialized re-run (EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md, 2026-07-19T23:05:27Z, current deployed commit d313d23) shows backend 828 passed/18 FAILED (17 of 18 traced to a resume-fixture regression from the same-commit 'time-bomb fixture fix'), frontend 463 passed/0 failed. Neither matches the claimed 676/0+297/0 nor any clean state. Console-error/same-origin-5xx sub-claim: CONFIRMED per-route across 15 independently-tested screens (0 console errors, 0 same-origin 5xx each), though the full literal 20-route sweep + E2E-suite-green portions were never performed as one single sweep. CORRECTION on finding coverage: MV-system-002 (fixCommits 952828f) covers a DIFFERENT, already-fixed defect (3 credential-resolution tests at baseline 53f0e08). It does NOT cover the CURRENT 18-test regression at commit d313d23 (EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md, 2026-07-19T23:05:27Z, root-caused to the same commit's "time-bomb fixture fix" breaking resume-fixture setup, MissingResumeError). Re-checked docs/delivery/MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json for any finding referencing this regression (time-bomb / d313d23 / the specific failing test names) -- zero hits. NEW FINDING NEEDED for the current 18-failure regression, distinct from MV-system-002.

claims.claim: A reserved-TLD email outside the allowlist (e.g. sub.aether.local) now correctly returns 422; an allowlisted domain (aether.local) returns 200 and persists (GATE-09)
claims.method: Attempt saving a non-allowlisted reserved-TLD email, confirm 422; attempt an allowlisted domain, confirm 200+persist
claims.adjudicationNote: Same evidence as CLM-012: sub.aether.local -> 422, aether.local -> 200+persisted, verified twice.

claims.claim: Sourcing yields >=2 sources x >=5 jobs each, fresh (GATE-10)
claims.method: GET /jobs on prod, group by source, count per-source volume and freshness (posting age)
claims.adjudicationNote: Fresh GET /jobs: ashby=8, lever=5, greenhouse=16 (>=3 sources each >=5), max posting age 29 days.

claims.claim: Fixture fingerprints are absent from live generation output (0/60 sampled) (GATE-14)
claims.method: Sample N live generations and check output text for known fixture-fingerprint strings
claims.findingNote: Cover-letter-studio's own tester flagged (UNSURE, not asserted as a confirmed refutation) that 2 PRE-EXISTING letters not generated by that tester were byte-identical to known fixture files. This was not independently re-verified by this auditor (would require DB/content access beyond this pass's scope) and has NO filed MV- finding. ESCALATING for orchestrator/qa-adversary follow-up verification -- if confirmed, this would be a live-fixture-serving defect requiring a new finding.
claims.adjudicationNote: 0/4 own live generations (resume-studio) and 0/4 own live generations (cover-letter-studio) matched known fixture fingerprints -- CONFIRMED for self-generated content, n=4 vs the claimed n=60 sample size. See findingNote for the one UNSURE counter-observation.

claims.claim: Zero non-prod code is reachable in the production build (GATE-18)
claims.method: Grep production build artifacts / deployed repo for prohibited patterns (mock/stub/TODO/placeholder reachable by users)
claims.adjudicationNote: Scoped grep of apps/web/src and apps/api/app (excluding tests/__tests__/fixtures): 0 Math.random() calls in reachable code (1 hit is a code comment documenting its absence), 0 TODO/FIXME in reachable src, 0 mock/stub markers. Spot-check, not exhaustive.

claims.claim: Zero orchestrator-tier (fable-5/opus-4-8) sub-agent spawns occurred during the Phase-7 run; model-tier routing held throughout (GATE-26)
claims.method: Governance/process claim -- not testable against live production UI; verifiable only via document/log review (out of scope for a live-behavior audit, recorded for completeness)
claims.adjudicationNote: Pure process/governance claim about sub-agent model-tier routing during a prior run. Its own verification method self-disqualifies as 'not testable against live production UI... out of scope for a live-behavior audit.'

claims.claim: Two LOW security incidents occurred where the operator's sk-ant-oat01- token and test/prod DATABASE_URLs were each echoed into a QA sub-agent's /tmp session transcript via diagnostic greps; neither is present in any committed file, artifact, or pushable path
claims.method: Grep git history and the repo working tree for the secret PREFIX only (first 8 chars max, never full value) to confirm no leakage into a committed/pushable path
claims.adjudicationNote: git grep + git log --all -p -S for the sk-ant-oat01- prefix pattern across all tracked history: 18 hits, every one is code/doc/test LOGIC referencing the prefix format (e.g. .startswith("sk-ant-oat01-")), not a leaked secret value. Grep for the production DB host substring in tracked files: 0 hits.

claims.claim: A prompt-injection attempt (an unsolicited external URL impersonating the user) appeared in one sub-agent's context during Phase-7 and was not acted upon
claims.method: Testimony only -- not independently re-derivable from current production state; verification method is UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI by nature of the claim
claims.adjudicationNote: Testimony-only claim about a specific sub-agent's internal context during a prior run. Its own verification method self-disqualifies as 'not independently re-derivable from current production state.'

claims.claim: At Phase-7 close: backend 676 pytest / 0 fail, frontend 297 vitest / 0 fail, tsc + build clean
claims.method: suite-run: pytest -q (apps/api) and pnpm -r test (apps/web) in the repo at the evaluated commit
claims.findingNote: MV-system-002 covers only the ORIGINAL 3-test issue (fixed). The CURRENT 18-test regression at d313d23 has NO finding -- NEW FINDING NEEDED (see EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md).
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-system-002: 3 credential-resolution tests were RED even at the clean Phase-7 baseline commit (53f0e08) -- the 676/0 claim was false as originally stated. This run's own fresh EXIT-G06 serialized re-run (2026-07-19T23:05:27Z, current commit d313d23) shows 828 passed/18 failed backend, 463 passed/0 failed frontend -- the suite has still never reproduced a clean 676/0+297/0 state matching the original claim, nor any 0-failure backend state at all as of the latest fresh check. CORRECTION on finding coverage: MV-system-002 (fixCommits 952828f) covers a DIFFERENT, already-fixed defect (3 credential-resolution tests at baseline 53f0e08). It does NOT cover the CURRENT 18-test regression at commit d313d23 (EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md, 2026-07-19T23:05:27Z, root-caused to the same commit's "time-bomb fixture fix" breaking resume-fixture setup, MissingResumeError). Re-checked docs/delivery/MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json for any finding referencing this regression (time-bomb / d313d23 / the specific failing test names) -- zero hits. NEW FINDING NEEDED for the current 18-failure regression, distinct from MV-system-002.

claims.claim: 23 of 29 Phase-6 gaps were VERIFIED-CLOSED on live production; 6 gaps BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN; 0 gaps OPEN
claims.method: Document cross-check against docs/delivery/phase6-gap-analysis.json; the underlying per-gap facts are captured as their own claims elsewhere in this ledger
claims.adjudicationNote: Historical meta-claim about Phase-6's own self-reported gap-closure tally (23/29/6/0). Not independently re-derivable from current live production as a single aggregate number; the individual gaps it summarizes are separately, freshly re-adjudicated as their own claims elsewhere in this ledger (several of which -- e.g. CLM-042, CLM-010 -- turned out false when re-audited in this run, which is circumstantial reason for caution about the reliability of any self-reported historical closure tally, but does not let this auditor independently recompute the specific 23/29/6/0 figures).

claims.claim: At Phase-6 close: backend 601 pytest / 0 fail, frontend 293 vitest / 0 fail
claims.method: suite-run (historical baseline, superseded by later 676/297 counts -- test current count instead)
claims.adjudicationNote: Historical point-in-time test-count snapshot (Phase-6 close: 601 pytest/293 vitest), explicitly flagged in its own overlap note as 'superseded by later 676/297 counts -- test current count instead.' Current baseline (EXIT-G06, 2026-07-19T23:05:27Z) is 828 passed/18 failed backend, 463/0 frontend -- neither the historical 601/0 nor any other single claimed snapshot has been reproduced clean at any re-check point in this run.

claims.claim: Sourcing (Phase-6): Seek scraping removed (ToS-compliant); Adzuna AU + ATS-API adapters live; 30 live jobs across 3 sources each >=5; 0 duplicates; 10/10 sampled cards live
claims.method: GET /jobs on prod: verify no Seek-sourced rows appear, count sources/freshness/dedup; sample 10 job card URLs for liveness (HTTP 200)
overlaps: docs/delivery/EXECUTION-REPORT.md:186-189 (Phase-6 EXECUTION-REPORT sourcing claim, near-identical numbers)
claims.adjudicationNote: Seek removal CONFIRMED (0 Seek rows live). Adzuna AU currently status=skipped (0 credentials configured, not live-contributing, contra 'ATS-API adapters live'). ATS adapters (greenhouse/lever/ashby) each >=5, live. 33 jobs across sources vs claimed 30 (natural drift upward, not a defect). 0 duplicates. 9/10 sampled cards live. MV-job-discovery-003 (ACCEPTED-DEVIATION, LOW) documents that the source-bar UI structurally diverges from the wireframe's named-consumer-board pattern -- accepted as-is, not filed for a fix.

claims.claim: Anthropic OAuth confirmed ToS-prohibited -> platform is API-key-only (ADR-P6-OAUTH); agent config PUT verified; multi-Gmail select_account verified in code
claims.method: Inspect agent-monitor UI for absence of an active interactive Anthropic-OAuth consent flow; PUT/GET a config round-trip; initiate a Gmail connect flow and confirm an account-selection (select_account) prompt appears
claims.adjudicationNote: All 3 sub-claims independently live-verified: (1) 404 on OAuth-start route; (2) live PUT/GET/restore round-trip on /agents/config/orchestration; (3) POST /emails/accounts/connect returned a real Google consent URL ending in prompt=select_account.

claims.claim: A silent fixture-fallback that served canned test fixtures as real LLM output on timeout (AUTH-002) was removed
claims.method: Attempt to force/observe a slow or failing LLM call and confirm an honest 503 (or async equivalent) is returned rather than a fixture; check output for known fixture-fingerprint text
claims.adjudicationNote: Genuine LLM-timeout/backend failures surfaced honest descriptive errors, zero fixture substitution, on both screens. MV-cover-letter-studio-005 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) confirms this is honest-but-was-too-internal-sounding, now fixed to a user-friendly message.

claims.claim: Production LLM mode is honest -- no fixture served; mode=auto (GATE-02)
claims.method: Check the reported LLM mode if a diagnostic surface exists, or infer from behavior (no fixture-fingerprint output under load)
claims.adjudicationNote: .env AETHER_LLM_MODE=auto (name+value confirmed fresh, non-secret). Corroborated by CLM-027/CLM-037: 0/8 sampled live generations across 2 screens matched any known fixture fingerprint, and apps/api/app/main.py refuses AETHER_LLM_MODE=replay outside explicitly-permitted contexts.

claims.claim: All 8 runtime agents (supervisor, scout, matcher, fitScorer, tailor, coverLetter, storyExtractor, emailAgent) are configurable; PUT persists their config (GATE-06)
claims.method: PUT a config change for each of the 8 runtime agents via the agent-monitor UI/API, GET back to confirm persistence
claims.adjudicationNote: All 8 backend agent keys confirmed present in GET /agents/config with identical schema. Only 1 of 8 (orchestration/supervisor) individually round-tripped live with a before/after/restore cycle, deliberately, to avoid mutating shared config mid-test for concurrent testers; the other 7 share the identical generic PUT handler per source inspection (no per-agent special-casing found).

claims.claim: Tailoring is content-only: strict ATS-score lift 32.97 > 30.81 baseline, with zero fabrication (GATE-09)
claims.method: Run tailor against a resume+job, compare before/after ATS score, inspect content for fabricated claims not traceable to source evidence
claims.adjudicationNote: Zero-fabrication CONFIRMED (guard rejected unsupported content in 3-4 of 4-5 runs). Positive-lift NOT reproduced: all 4 live runs showed exactly 0.0% ATS delta, not the claimed 32.97>30.81 lift. MV-resume-studio-003 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) additionally found and fixed a related defect: when the guard rejects 100% of proposed edits, the system still created and billed a byte-identical, indistinguishably-labeled 'Tailored' version.

claims.claim: ATS score is shown with a MetricTooltip, methodology text, and before/after values (GATE-10)
claims.method: UI inspection: hover/click the MetricTooltip on the ATS score, confirm methodology text and before/after numbers render
claims.adjudicationNote: ATS Conversion Impact panel with MetricTooltip, methodology text, and before/after values all confirmed live in the DOM.

claims.claim: Cover letter generation reliably completes in business format; craft score 78 (GATE-11)
claims.method: Generate a cover letter and inspect structure (sender block/date/recipient/salutation/paragraphs/CTA/sign-off)
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-cover-letter-studio-001 (BLOCKER, VERIFIED-CLOSED): every generated cover letter opened with a grammatically broken hook sentence ('My background as an I am a direct match...'), reproduced identically across 3 independent jobs. MV-cover-letter-studio-002 (BLOCKER, VERIFIED-CLOSED): every 'Request Changes'/'Regenerate' refine call produced a structurally duplicated letter (salutation/hook/closing each doubled). Both fixed at e7ba5b9, qa-adversary-reverified fresh on prod. The tester's own verdict was explicitly REFUTED (100%-reproducible defects directly contradict 'reliably completes in business format').

claims.claim: Cover-letter PDF export is a clean single-page PDF (GATE-12)
claims.method: Download the cover-letter PDF, inspect page count and rendering quality
claims.adjudicationNote: pdfinfo confirms 1-page A4 valid PDF, 200 OK -- but only for a non-corrupted draft; not re-verified for a duplicated-salutation draft (see CLM-042), which would likely span more than one clean page.

claims.claim: Admin flows -- spend-cap-429-before-LLM, user suspend, signup toggle, audit log -- are functionality-verified live via a temporary admin account; formal closure needs operator-rotated admin credential (GATE-17)
claims.method: Requires an admin session -- if no operator admin credential is configured, at minimum confirm /admin/* endpoints reject unauthenticated/non-admin requests (401/403); full flow test needs admin access (may be UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI without one)
claims.adjudicationNote: 'Formal closure blocked' half CONFIRMED (operator credentials AETHER_ADMIN_EMAIL/AETHER_ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH still absent, admin/admin123 still isAdmin:false, live-reconfirmed across 5 admin screens). 'Verified live via a temporary admin account' half describes PRIOR-PHASE testimony this run's testers had no credential to reproduce -- UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI for that half.

claims.claim: Analytics metrics UI matches a user-scoped SQL recomputation with 0.0% delta (GATE-18)
claims.method: Compare displayed analytics numbers against a direct DB query for the same user (per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md sanctioned DB access)
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-analytics-005 (VERIFIED-CLOSED): direct SQL recomputation confirms both displayed numbers (dashboard-summary 'Applications'=14, funnel 'Applied'=7) are individually real, non-fabricated, 0.0% delta vs DB -- the underlying data-accuracy portion of this claim CONFIRMS. The defect found+fixed was presentation-only (no disambiguation between the two different, both-correct, application counts). MV-analytics-001 (BLOCKER, CONDITIONALLY-CLOSED-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN, ADR-MV-02 pending) documents that the whole analytics screen is paywalled for a free-plan account -- a related access-scope caveat, not a data-accuracy defect against this specific claim.

claims.claim: E2E: 17 core screens verified live + cover-503 fixed + mobile-overflow fixed; /pipeline/run is edge-limited (524 @125s) as a documented async residual (BACKLOG-P6-02) as of Phase-6 (GATE-26)
claims.method: Trigger POST /agents/pipeline/run on prod; per later Phase-7 claims (async now permanently on) this residual should be RESOLVED -- confirm no 524 timeout and instead a 202 async response
claims.adjudicationNote: The specific 524-timeout symptom is confirmed gone on 3 screens: POST /agents/pipeline/run now returns 202 in well under 1s (fastest 0.22s observed), never a 125s edge timeout. Full async success path corroborated by CLM-014's 5+ completed enqueue->poll->terminal cycles.

claims.claim: admin/admin123 login shows isAdmin=false in production, and is unconditionally demoted on every boot regardless of environment (GATE-31)
claims.method: Attempt login with admin/admin123 on prod, inspect isAdmin field in the session/me response
claims.adjudicationNote: isAdmin:false CONFIRMED (GET /auth/me, reproduced twice). The 'unconditionally demoted on every boot' MECHANISM claim requires an aether-api.service restart to observe directly, which is prohibited for a screen-tester (no service restarts) -- UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI for that half. UPDATE (2026-07-20T00:37:54Z, this auditor's own independent fresh probe + read-only DB SELECT, see uat/reports/evidence/manual-verification/claims/probes/CRITICAL-canonical-admin-account-missing-20260720T003958Z.md): admin/admin123 no longer AUTHENTICATES AT ALL (401 "Invalid email or password"); direct DB SELECT confirms 0 of 19 current User rows match admin/admin@aether.local -- the account has been deleted from production entirely, not merely demoted. The isAdmin:false CONFIRMED-tier evidence above remains valid as of when it was captured (2026-07-17), but as of this moment the underlying account plainly no longer exists, which is a stronger and more current fact superseding the narrower isAdmin:false framing. Flagged for urgent orchestrator escalation -- see the dedicated incident probe.

claims.claim: Rate limiting returns HTTP 429 after 5 requests on the auth endpoint (GATE-32)
claims.method: Send 6+ rapid login attempts and confirm 429 after the 5th
claims.adjudicationNote: Reproduced twice: 5 failed logins -> 401 each, 6th -> 429 with Retry-After (~899s); server log independently shows 4 matching 429s in the test window.

claims.claim: All users are backfilled to the Free plan with a quota row (GATE-34)
claims.method: Check /billing/entitlement for an existing/fresh user account, confirm Free-plan default and a quota row exists
claims.adjudicationNote: Direct DB read: all 5 current User rows each have exactly 1 Subscription row and 1 UsageQuota row (0 missing). Pricing screen corroborates for its own scoped account.

claims.claim: BACKLOG-P6-01: no per-run Cost column is surfaced in the /dashboard/agents Recent Runs table -- only an aggregate avg-cost stat is shown
claims.method: Inspect the Recent Runs table UI on /dashboard/agents for presence/absence of a per-run Cost column
claims.adjudicationNote: Live Recent Runs table headers (both sessions): AGENT, STATUS, STARTED, ERROR -- no Cost column present.

claims.claim: The Phase-6 re-run found 0 regressions, 0 new gaps, 0 code fixes required -- production reproduces the first Phase-6 run's verified-closed state
claims.method: Aggregate meta-claim; verified via the individual re-derived gate claims captured separately in this ledger
claims.adjudicationNote: Aggregate meta-claim about Phase-6-rerun's own internal before/after comparison. Not independently re-derivable as a single number from current live production; its constituent gate-level claims are separately re-adjudicated elsewhere in this ledger, several of which (e.g. CLM-042, CLM-010, CLM-024/032) were found REFUTED when re-audited in this exhaustive pass -- reason for caution about any prior run's self-reported '0 regressions,' but not proof that Phase-6-rerun's own narrower comparison was itself wrong.

claims.claim: At Phase-6-rerun: backend 627 pytest / 0 failed, frontend 296 vitest / 0 failed
claims.method: suite-run (historical baseline, superseded by Phase-7's 676/297 -- test current count instead)
claims.adjudicationNote: Historical point-in-time snapshot (Phase-6-rerun: 627 pytest/296 vitest), explicitly flagged in its own overlap note as superseded. See CLM-034 reasoning -- current baseline (828/18 backend, 463/0 frontend, 2026-07-19T23:05:27Z) matches none of the historical snapshots.

claims.claim: The subscription paywall (AETHER_REQUIRE_PAID_SUBSCRIPTION=true) is ACTIVE in production -- an unpaid agent run attempt returns HTTP 402 subscription_required
claims.method: Attempt an agent run as an unpaid/free-tier user on prod, confirm 402 with subscription_required error code
claims.adjudicationNote: Multiple independent fresh 402 subscription_required confirmations for unpaid-account agent-run attempts (job-discovery scout/run, pricing tailor/scout/cover-letter, resume-studio fresh throwaway account + dashboard paywall). The paywall mechanism itself is unambiguously active and honest. (MV-pricing-001 documents a DISTINCT, still-open marketing-honesty problem about what the Free tier's pricing card claims to include -- see CLM-097/CLM-094 notes -- not a contradiction of the paywall's own mechanical correctness.)

claims.claim: Production shows 32 jobs across 5 sources with 0 duplicates; billing/admin DB tables and User.isAdmin/suspended columns are present; 8 runtime agents confirmed; auth rate-limit 429-after-5 confirmed; the billing webhook rejects an unsigned payload with HTTP 400
claims.method: GET /jobs and count sources/dedup; POST an unsigned payload to the webhook endpoint expecting 400
claims.adjudicationNote: Jobs/source/dedup CONFIRMED close to claim (33 jobs/5 sources/0 dup vs claimed 32/5/0 -- natural live-board drift). '8 runtime agents' CONFIRMED via sidebar widget. DB-table-presence, auth-rate-limit, and webhook sub-claims are out of this screen's scope but independently CONFIRMED elsewhere in this ledger (rate-limit: CLM-048; webhook: CLM-095).

claims.claim: Code inventory (Phase-6-rerun) confirms: paywall check (_require_active_subscription) runs first in the run-recording path; spend-cap-before-LLM enforcement present; 0 prohibited patterns found in the codebase; the fixture-fallback function (_replay_with_default) is absent; anthropic_oauth.py module is absent; GmailAccount multi-inbox model present
claims.method: Largely a source-code-read claim, not directly UI-drivable; the practically live-testable subset (paywall-first, 0 prohibited patterns, no interactive OAuth) is covered by claims 9, 11, 29, 54, 90 in this ledger -- treat the exact source-line assertions as code claims verifiable only by reading the deployed repo
claims.adjudicationNote: Source-verified directly by this auditor: anthropic_oauth.py absent from the source tree (only a stale compiled .pyc remains); _replay_with_default fixture-fallback function absent from apps/api/app/ entirely; GmailAccount table (userId+accountEmail unique index, isPrimary partial index) present, confirming the multi-inbox model; _require_active_subscription is called at the top of every run-recording path (agents.py:612,1000,1060) before any LLM dispatch; the USD spend-cap check (spend_cap_exceeded) fires at the same pre-dispatch reservation point (line 630-642); 0 prohibited patterns found per CLM-028's scoped grep.

claims.claim: Under a temporary paid subscription: POST /agents/scout/run returns 202 with honest per-source status (e.g. Wellfound 403 surfaced, not swallowed); GET /jobs returns 32 jobs, 3 sources each >=5 (greenhouse 16/ashby 7/lever 5), max posting age 28 days, 0 dup, 0 Seek-origin rows
claims.method: Requires a paid-subscription test context; trigger scout run, inspect per-source status; GET /jobs and count/group by source
claims.adjudicationNote: Live scout/run under a temp paid entitlement returned 202 with honest per-source status (Wellfound 403 surfaced, not swallowed); GET /jobs showed greenhouse=16, lever=5 exact match to claim, ashby=8 (claim 7, minor drift), 0 dup, 0 Seek.

claims.claim: 10/10 sampled job cards return HTTP 200 with 0 expiry markers, 100% title-token match, 0 Seek-sourced cards (Phase-6-rerun GATE-08)
claims.method: Sample 10 live job card source URLs, curl each for HTTP 200 and absence of expiry/closed-posting text
claims.adjudicationNote: 9/10 sampled job-card URLs returned HTTP 200; 1/10 returned 403 (a bot-block, not an expiry marker, so not a contradiction of live-freshness per se). 0/10 Seek-sourced cards.

claims.claim: 4 live tailoring runs were content-only + non-regression on ATS + zero fabrication; the entailment guard actively rejects unsupported proposed candidate bullets rather than passing them through
claims.method: Run tailor multiple times against different JDs, inspect diffs for fabricated/unsupported content, confirm ATS score does not regress
claims.adjudicationNote: 5 live tailor invocations: ATS score never regressed across any run; the entailment guard visibly rejected unsupported bullets in 4 of 5 runs.

claims.claim: Conversion UI: estimatedConversionLift + methodology text + a working MetricTooltip (data-testid trigger/popover, role=tooltip, hover-revealed, illustrative-estimate disclaimer) render in the real DOM; before/after ATS is shown; 0 console errors (GATE-10)
claims.method: Playwright: inspect DOM for the data-testid tooltip trigger/popover with role=tooltip attribute; hover to reveal; check disclaimer text; capture console errors
claims.adjudicationNote: estimatedConversionLift + methodology text + role=tooltip popover with the exact 'illustrative estimate' disclaimer + before/after ATS values all confirmed live in the DOM on resume-studio (where this component actually lives), with 0 console errors across 2 sessions. The Analytics screen's own separate instance of similar UI is currently blocked for free-plan accounts (MV-analytics-001, CONDITIONALLY-CLOSED-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN) -- a distinct access-scope issue for that specific screen, not a refutation of this claim which is independently satisfied at resume-studio.

claims.claim: A live cover-letter run was confirmed via rendered PDF: sender block / date / recipient / salutation / 3 paragraphs / CTA / sign-off (GATE-11)
claims.method: Generate a cover letter, download the PDF, inspect structural elements
claims.adjudicationNote: Structural skeleton (sender block/date/recipient/salutation/paragraphs/CTA/sign-off) present and exported to a clean single-page PDF for a non-corrupted draft. But the same generation mechanism reliably corrupts the hook sentence on initial generation and duplicates structure on refine (see CLM-042) -- 'confirmed' quality is overstated by the original claim.

claims.claim: Every factual claim in a sampled cover letter traces to source evidence (zero hallucinated facts); the exported PDF is a valid single page (HTTP 200) (GATE-12)
claims.method: Generate and manually inspect a cover letter's content against story-bank/resume source evidence for fabrication
claims.adjudicationNote: Quantifiable facts in sampled letters trace cleanly to Story Bank source evidence -- CONFIRMED. But 'zero hallucinated facts' as an absolute claim is REFUTED-in-part: MV-cover-letter-studio-004 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) found a refined letter signed with a fabricated candidate name ('Vikram Deshpande') appearing nowhere in the account's resume; MV-cover-letter-studio-003 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) found embedded prompt-injection text leaking verbatim into a sendable draft. Both fixed. PDF export itself CONFIRMED valid.

claims.claim: A Playwright sweep of 14 dashboard routes + /pricing + /admin as a paid admin showed 0 console errors / 0 failed requests / 0 page errors (GATE-03)
claims.method: Playwright sweep of the listed routes on prod, capture console + network errors
claims.adjudicationNote: 14 of the 16 named routes were each independently confirmed clean (0 console errors, 0 failed requests, 0 page errors) by their own screen-tester under whatever account state was available to them. But the claim's specific precondition -- 'as a paid admin' -- could not be reproduced by any tester (no admin credential exists in production; see CLM-044), and no single sweep covering all 16 routes in one paid-admin session was performed. story-bank additionally noted 4 non-user-facing net::ERR_ABORTED events on DELETE calls (a strict-reading caveat, not a user-facing failure).

claims.claim: After Phase-6-rerun's paid-subscription test cleanup, production was restored exactly: entitlement.active_paid=false, POST /agents/scout/run returns 402 again, and the dashboard shows the paywall again
claims.method: Check current entitlement state for the test account and confirm the paywall is showing (does not require mutating production billing state -- read-only check)
claims.adjudicationNote: Current entitlement state for the test account: active_paid:false; POST /agents/scout/run -> 402; dashboard shows the paywall -- exactly as claimed, reproduced across 2 sessions. MV-dashboard-003 (CONDITIONALLY-CLOSED-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN) documents a related-but-distinct coverage-gap (the paywall prevents exercising ~90% of the dashboard hub's own wiring under this account), not a refutation of this specific restored-paywall-state claim.

claims.claim: One genuine transient HTTP 503 occurred during a UI-driven tailor run ("LLM call exceeded hard budget of 38.7s"); an immediate retry succeeded
claims.method: One-off historical observation, not deterministically reproducible; best-effort soak reproduction only -- likely to end UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI unless a fresh transient 503 is independently observed
claims.adjudicationNote: A fresh transient 503 ('LLM call exceeded hard budget') was reproduced matching the genuine-never-fixture pattern (holds). But the immediate retry ALSO failed both times in this run's observation, differing from the historical claim that 'an immediate retry succeeded' -- likely an artifact of concurrent-load timing rather than a code regression, but the specific historical claim doesn't hold as re-tested.

claims.claim: The /admin route redirects cleanly (no crash) when visited without an isAdmin=true session, because no DB user currently has admin privileges
claims.method: Visit /admin without an admin session, confirm a clean redirect rather than an error page
claims.adjudicationNote: Both unauthenticated->/login and demoted-authenticated->/dashboard redirects confirmed clean, 0 console errors, 0 crashes, reproduced twice each.

claims.claim: Seek's robots.txt explicitly names anthropic-ai as disallowed; 10/10 sampled Seek-sourced job cards returned HTTP 403
claims.method: Fetch Seek's robots.txt directly and check for an anthropic-ai disallow rule; confirm no Seek-origin job currently appears live in /jobs
claims.adjudicationNote: Fresh fetch of au.seek.com/robots.txt confirms an anthropic-ai disallow rule -- CONFIRMED. The '10/10 sampled Seek-sourced cards returned 403' sub-claim is UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI as currently stated: 0 Seek-sourced jobs exist live to sample (Seek was removed from sourcing entirely, per CLM-035), so there is nothing to 403-test against today.

claims.claim: 6 dead view-toggle controls (GAP-P6-WIRE-001) were identified pre-fix and are claimed fixed/wired
endpoints: (TBD - exact controls not named in this doc; identify via probe-06 or live UI inspection of view-toggle controls)
claims.method: UI inspection: locate view-toggle controls referenced by probe-06, click each, confirm it changes displayed state -- likely needs additional discovery before a definitive verdict is possible
claims.adjudicationNote: Live click-through of 30+ view-toggle-style controls found 0 dead controls present-day (good news, not a defect). The historical count of '6 dead controls' pre-fix is itself unconfirmable now (no way to re-observe a pre-fix state).

claims.claim: Stripe billing round-trip (checkout, webhook, GST invoice, ABN/Tax) is BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN pending operator-supplied Stripe test keys, webhook secret, Price IDs, and ABN/Tax enrollment (GATE-13/14/15/16/33)
claims.method: Attempt POST /billing/checkout on prod; observe whether it succeeds (would indicate Stripe now configured, contradicting this claim) or fails/degrades honestly (would confirm still-blocked)
claims.adjudicationNote: All three findings VERIFIED-CLOSED: MV-pricing-003 found no subscription-management/downgrade/portal UI existed anywhere despite the backend endpoints working, fixed (430f87f); MV-pricing-004 found non-401/503 checkout errors (incl. the 429 rate-limiter) collapsed into one generic message with no Retry-After surfaced, fixed (14c2a72); MV-settings-003 found 3 of 4 assigned billing endpoints were never called by Settings, fixed (430f87f). Current fresh probes confirm the 'honest degrade, never fabricated' half: checkout -> honest 400 'no Stripe price configured', portal -> honest 409 'No billing account yet'. Full live Stripe round-trip remains HUMAN-GATED per PHASE6-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN.md (no operator Stripe test keys).

claims.claim: A second real Gmail account can be connected via "Connect Gmail" on /dashboard/email once human OAuth-consent prerequisites are met -- this remains pending as of the source doc (only 1 account connected)
claims.method: GET /emails/accounts on prod, count connected Gmail accounts (expect 1 if still-blocked, 2+ if since resolved)
claims.adjudicationNote: GET /emails/accounts returns exactly 1 connected account, matching the still-blocked expectation; the 'Add Gmail Account' mechanism itself is confirmed functional and correctly routes to a real Google consent flow. H-6 corroborates: multi-inbox would need a second real human consent, not filed as a defect.

claims.claim: Admin credential rotation off admin/admin123 (AETHER_ADMIN_EMAIL + bcrypt AETHER_ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH) is pending as of the source doc -- GATE-17/31 formal closure blocked
claims.method: Attempt login with admin/admin123; separately check whether any other operator-configured admin credential is now active/functional
claims.adjudicationNote: admin/admin123 isAdmin:false portion CONFIRMED (same evidence as CLM-047). Whether any OTHER operator-configured admin credential is now active, and whether GATE-17/31 formal closure has since happened, is UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI -- no candidate alternate credential exists to test, and per protocol testers will not guess/brute-force one. UPDATE (2026-07-20T00:37:54Z, this auditor's own independent fresh probe + read-only DB SELECT, see uat/reports/evidence/manual-verification/claims/probes/CRITICAL-canonical-admin-account-missing-20260720T003958Z.md): admin/admin123 has gone beyond "non-admin" to "nonexistent" -- 0 of 19 current User rows match. No alternate operator credential exists either (still 0 isAdmin=true rows in the whole table). Formal GATE-17/31 closure remains blocked, now compounded by the loss of even the non-admin seed account.

claims.claim: Adzuna AU API credentials are optional and absent; without them the sourcing floor (GATE-07, >=25 jobs) is still met via ATS + public APIs, but with a thin/decaying fresh-jobs margin
claims.method: Count live jobs by source on prod, confirm the >=25 threshold currently holds and whether Adzuna contributes 0 jobs
claims.adjudicationNote: Adzuna AU confirmed skipped/0 credentials configured; live job count (33) still exceeds the >=25 floor, with a thin margin as claimed (remoteok=3, remotive=1 contributing very little).

claims.claim: The résumé asset assets/resume/Vik_Resume_Final.pdf remains byte-for-byte unchanged (md5 16b856c0f3f4ec0d801fdde6d084452c), used as an unmodified format-conformance reference
claims.method: Compute md5 of assets/resume/Vik_Resume_Final.pdf in the repo, compare to the claimed hash (repo file check, not a live-UI check)
claims.adjudicationNote: md5 of assets/resume/Vik_Resume_Final.pdf computed fresh by the tester: exact match to the claimed 16b856c0f3f4ec0d801fdde6d084452c.

claims.claim: Approving or rejecting an approval request synchronizes the linked application's status (approve->submitted, reject->rejected), with a draft-only guard
claims.method: Create/observe an approval request in draft state, approve it, confirm the linked application's status changes to submitted; repeat for reject->rejected
claims.adjudicationNote: Draft-only guard clause held both directions, reproduced twice. Approve->submitted transition directly live-verified twice (timestamped reads). Reject->rejected transition verified via source-code confirmation of the shared _sync_application code path (4 attempts to generate a fresh linked letter for a live re-test hit transient 503s, so the reject path's live re-trigger is INFERRED from the shared code path rather than freshly self-triggered this run).

claims.claim: Every job card on job-discovery has a "Tailor Resume ->" deep link to /dashboard/resume?job=<id>
claims.method: UI inspection: click the Tailor link on a job card, confirm navigation to Resume Studio with the job pre-selected
claims.adjudicationNote: Deep-link mechanics fully confirmed working: clicking the job card's resume-studio link navigates to /dashboard/resume?job=<id> and correctly pre-selects the job. But the literal button label is 'Preview' / 'Open in Resume Studio' in the live UI, not literally 'Tailor Resume ->' as the claim states -- a cosmetic label mismatch, not a functional defect.

claims.claim: Resume Studio has a Download button for resumes
claims.method: Click Download on a resume in the UI, confirm the file downloads (or, if still unimplemented, that it degrades gracefully rather than erroring silently)
claims.adjudicationNote: Clicked Download on a live resume; received a valid 189KB PDF file.

claims.claim: Story Bank supports manual create/edit of STAR-format stories via a form
claims.method: Create a new story via the UI form, confirm persistence; edit an existing story, confirm the update saves
claims.adjudicationNote: Live create and edit of a STAR-format story via the UI form, persisted across a full page reload, reproduced in 2 independent sessions.

claims.claim: Approval requests show a 48-hour expiry badge, with actions disabled once expired
claims.method: Inspect approval UI for an expiry badge; this is data-dependent (needs an approval near/past 48h) and may be UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI if no such live data exists
claims.adjudicationNote: 48-hour expiry badge and disabled actions confirmed at the card, modal, and backend (direct POST -> 409) layers on 2 screens, reproduced twice.

claims.claim: Analytics funnel/conversion-rate stage percentages render and re-fetch when the period filter changes
claims.method: Change the period filter on the analytics screen, confirm the funnel/conversion numbers update
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-analytics-004 (VERIFIED-CLOSED): confirms the Application Funnel and Stage Conversion panels (this claim's literal scope) DO correctly re-fetch and re-render on period-filter change. The finding's underlying defect was that OTHER, separately-labeled panels (dashboard-summary cards, ATS distribution, Agent ROI) on the same page do NOT respect the period filter -- a real but distinct gap outside this claim's literal 'funnel/conversion-rate stage percentages' scope, now fixed (30f36e0) and qa-adversary-reverified fresh on prod.

claims.claim: GET/POST /api/agents/auth/anthropic/start returns 404 -- the consumer-subscription OAuth start endpoint was removed end-to-end; the x-api-key credential path remains intact
claims.method: curl GET and POST to this path on prod, expect 404
claims.adjudicationNote: Same live evidence as CLM-011; re-confirmed fresh by this auditor's own curl probe (GET 404, POST 404).

claims.claim: A per-source status endpoint/surface returns honest per-source status distinguishing genuine-zero results from a total outage (e.g. Wellfound status=error for a real 403; Indeed/LinkedIn status=skipped), never a silent errors:[] masking failures
endpoints: (TBD - per-source status endpoint not explicitly named in SCREEN-MATRIX; likely surfaced via GET /jobs or a dedicated /agents/scout/sources-style endpoint -- confirm exact path during probe)
claims.method: Trigger a scout run and inspect the per-source status response/UI panel for honest status codes per source
claims.adjudicationNote: Fresh GET /agents/scout/sources: wellfound=error with the real 403 error text; indeed/linkedin=skipped; no silent errors:[] masking observed.

claims.claim: A per-source "Sync Status" panel is shown on /dashboard/jobs, displaying honest ok/error/skipped badges matching the API 1:1
claims.method: UI inspection of the job-discovery source bar (source-bar-jd22 per wireframe), compare displayed badges to the underlying API response
claims.adjudicationNote: Exact 1:1 field match between the live UI Sync Status panel and the underlying API response across all 10 sources, including the verbatim Wellfound error string.

claims.claim: Tailored resume ATS score is >= baseline (non-negative lift) -- a previously catastrophic negative-lift regression was reversed
claims.method: Run tailor, compare before/after ATS scores, confirm tailored >= baseline
claims.adjudicationNote: All 4 live tailor runs showed exactly 0.0% ATS delta -- technically satisfies 'non-negative' (weak/literal form of the claim) but never demonstrated a positive lift as the claim's headline framing implies.

claims.claim: Cover letter generation produces a business-letter structure with a specific role/company/program hook, JD-matched grounded evidence, a specific CTA, first-person voice throughout, honest non-boastful tone; all quantifiers are verbatim-grounded to source evidence
claims.method: Generate a cover letter, manually audit structure/tone/grounding against story-bank/resume source data
claims.adjudicationNote: Role/company/JD-matched-evidence/CTA/first-person voice all present when generation is not corrupted. 'Honest non-boastful tone' is undermined by the hallucinated-sign-off-name (CLM-061) and duplicate-structure (CLM-042) defects found in this same generation mechanism -- both since fixed.

claims.claim: The Wellfound sourcing adapter is 403-blocked from the production VM, surfaced honestly as status=error (not silently skipped or faked)
claims.method: Trigger a scout run, inspect Wellfound's per-source status for status=error
claims.adjudicationNote: Wellfound's real 403 surfaced honestly as status=error, reproduced twice.

claims.claim: The Indeed/LinkedIn sourcing adapters are fixture-only (no live mode) and are reported as status=skipped, never faked as live results
claims.method: Trigger a scout run, inspect Indeed/LinkedIn per-source status for status=skipped
claims.adjudicationNote: Indeed/LinkedIn consistently reported status=skipped (lastFetched=0) across 2 separate scout runs, never faked as live results.

claims.claim: The root path / redirects (HTTP 307) to /dashboard
claims.method: curl -I https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/ and check for a 307 redirect to /dashboard
claims.adjudicationNote: curl -I -> HTTP/2 307, location:/dashboard; independently reproduced via a Playwright request with maxRedirects:0.

claims.claim: An unmapped /dashboard/<unknown> subroute returns a generic placeholder (HTTP 200), not a 404 -- via the [...slug] catch-all
claims.method: Visit an arbitrary /dashboard/nonexistent-xyz path, confirm 200 with fallback content rather than a 404
claims.adjudicationNote: HTTP 200 (not 404) confirmed for an unmapped /dashboard/<unknown> subroute -- that narrow part holds. But MV-dashboard-001 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) found the rendered content for a free-plan account was the generic SubscriptionGate paywall, not the [...slug] route's OWN 'Section not found' placeholder as the claim specifically describes -- so the user got no signal the specific URL didn't exist. Fixed at 30f36e0, qa-adversary-reverified fresh on prod.

claims.claim: A spend cap of $0 set on a user blocks any agent run before the LLM call is dispatched: the user receives an honest HTTP 429 and their AgentRun count stays at zero
claims.method: Requires admin access: set a $0 spend cap on a test user, trigger an agent run as that user, confirm 429 and AgentRun count unchanged
claims.adjudicationNote: Requires setting another user's $0 spend cap via POST /admin/users/{id}/spend-cap, which is admin-only. No operator-admin credential exists in production (confirmed absent across every admin screen this run); the only available credential (admin/admin123, isAdmin:false) returns 403 on this endpoint. HUMAN-GATED.

claims.claim: All 10 /admin/* routes are gated server-side on isAdmin: unauthenticated requests get 401, non-admin authenticated requests get 403 -- never client-side-only trust
claims.method: curl each /admin/* endpoint with no auth (expect 401) and with a non-admin authenticated session (expect 403)
claims.adjudicationNote: admin-root: 20/20 unauthenticated calls -> 401, 20/20 demoted-authenticated calls -> 403 across 10 endpoints x2 passes; raw-HTML no-JS check confirms zero server-side data leak before any client guard runs. This auditor's own fresh probe corroborates: 5/5 admin GET endpoints -> 401 unauthenticated. MV-admin-settings-002 (VERIFIED-CLOSED, LOW severity) documents one narrow, non-exploitable edge case: a syntactically-invalid JSON body from an unauthenticated caller gets a 422 (FastAPI body-parse ordering) instead of 401 on POST /admin/settings specifically -- no data disclosure results, standard framework dependency-resolution behavior, not treated as contradicting the claim's substance.

claims.claim: A fresh live scout run yielded 30 active-feed jobs across 5 sources (up from a 6-job/4-source pre-fix baseline), 100% fresh <=30 days, 0 duplicates, 0 Seek rows; 10/10 sampled job cards independently confirmed live
claims.method: GET /jobs on prod, count sources/freshness/dedup; sample 10 job cards for liveness
claims.adjudicationNote: The live dataset was already at 33 jobs/5 sources before this run's own scout trigger, so the historical '6-job/4-source pre-fix baseline -> 30/5' delta itself is not independently reproducible now. Freshness (100% <=30 days), 0 duplicates, and 0 Seek rows all CONFIRMED fresh; 9/10 sampled job cards independently confirmed live.

claims.claim: An entailment-verification pass reverts a tailored bullet with an unsupported claim rather than shipping it; a top-8 batch cap + scaled entailment budget resolves a "verifier starves the shared latency budget" defect
claims.method: Run tailor against a JD requiring unsupported claims to fill gaps, confirm the guard reverts them rather than fabricating
claims.adjudicationNote: Live rejected arrays of exactly 8 entries observed on 2 separate runs, matching the documented top-8 batch cap; the entailment guard visibly reverted unsupported proposed bullets rather than shipping them.

claims.claim: A fresh 10-attempt live production QA round showed 8/10 honest completions (2 of which delivered a genuine ATS-score lift, 30.81->32.97) with zero fabrication survivors across all 8 completions; the remaining ~20% returned an honest HTTP 503 (never a fixture) due to the synchronous ~100s HTTP edge
claims.method: Run N tailor attempts on prod; per later Phase-7 claims (async now permanently enabled, claim-3/14/17) the ~20% honest-503 rate should now be RESOLVED to ~0% -- test whether this superseded residual still applies
claims.adjudicationNote: 'Honest 503, never a fixture' portion CONFIRMED (0/4 fixture matches; ~0% observed 503 rate this run, an improvement matching the async-permanently-on claims). The 'delivered a genuine ATS-score lift (2 of the 10 attempts)' portion was NOT reproduced: 0 of 4 fresh live attempts delivered a positive lift (all exactly 0.0% delta) -- weaker than the original claim's specific 2/10 figure.

claims.claim: Cover-letter fabrication guarding catches JD-title-echoed and capitalized-entity claims but not pure narrative/causal-embellishment claims that reuse no JD/evidence vocabulary; the mandatory human-approval gate before sending is the backstop for that residual
claims.method: Attempt to observe a narrative-embellishment-style hallucination in generated cover-letter output (predicted NOT to be caught by the automated guard); separately confirm a human-approval step exists before any cover letter is sent
claims.adjudicationNote: The mandatory human-approval gate before sending is CONFIRMED to exist and function. But the claim's more specific framing ('the guard catches JD-title-echoed and capitalized-entity claims') is REFUTED-in-part: MV-cover-letter-studio-003 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) shows an injected capitalized token ('COMELY') sailed through into a saved, exportable, sendable draft with only a passive post-hoc advisory tag, not a block. MV-approval-modal-001 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) additionally found the approval review modal doesn't even render the letter text for real agent-generated approvals, undermining the 'human backstop' framing further -- a defective letter (broken hook sentence, MV-approval-modal-009) sailed through an approval where the reviewer literally could not see what they were approving. All fixed at e7ba5b9 / 7a5448f, qa-adversary-reverified fresh on prod.

claims.claim: Subscription pricing: 4 tiers -- Free A$0 / Starter A$19 / Pro A$39 / Power A$69 monthly; A$179/359/649 annual; GST-inclusive AUD with gst = round(total/11, 2)
claims.method: GET /billing/plans on prod (or view /pricing), confirm tier names/prices match; compute GST via the stated formula on a sample total and compare to the displayed value
claims.adjudicationNote: Tier names, monthly/annual prices, and GST math (gst=round(total/11,2)) all CONFIRMED to exact match on 2 screens via live GET /billing/plans + UI cross-check. MV-pricing-002 (VERIFIED-CLOSED) separately found the pricing page's per-plan 'model tier' marketing copy (LIGHT/STANDARD/ADVANCED/PREMIUM) had no backing implementation -- every plan resolves the same fixed model per task-type, uniformly. Frontend copy fixed (14c2a72); a backend follow-up to remove the stale claim from billing.py's own feature list was noted as still outstanding in the finding.

claims.claim: The Stripe webhook handler reads the raw request body strictly before signature verification, strictly before any parsing; StripeEvent insert-in-transaction idempotency ensures a replayed webhook event creates no second entitlement
claims.method: Live Stripe webhook verification requires operator-supplied Stripe test keys (human-gated per PHASE6-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN.md); at minimum, confirm an unsigned/malformed webhook payload is rejected with 400 as a partial check
claims.adjudicationNote: Fresh curl POST with a JSON body and no stripe-signature header -> HTTP 400 {"detail":"Missing stripe-signature header"} -- CONFIRMED for this minimum sub-claim. The raw-body-before-parsing internals and replay-idempotency-via-StripeEvent behavior remain UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI without real operator-supplied Stripe test keys (PHASE6-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN.md).

claims.claim: Quota is reserved atomically before an agent run executes, with refund-on-failure; deterministic agents (scout/fitScorer/matcher/supervisor) are unmetered and do not consume quota
claims.method: Check quota/usage before and after running scout (expect unchanged, unmetered) vs running tailor (expect decremented)
claims.adjudicationNote: 'Unmetered' half CONFIRMED live on 2 screens: every completed scout/matcher/fitScorer/supervisor run shows costUsd:0.0 and billingAudit.quotaPath:none. 'Atomic reserve+refund for metered agents' half is source-confirmed only (agents.py:966-990), not self-triggered live (paywall-blocked test accounts).

claims.claim: The /pricing page exists and is publicly viewable (unauthenticated)
claims.method: Visit /pricing unauthenticated, confirm the page renders with plan info
claims.adjudicationNote: Unauthenticated load of /pricing returns HTTP 200 with full real plan content, no redirect. (MV-pricing-001's claimRef association to this claim concerns a distinct, still-open issue -- whether the Free tier's advertised FEATURES are honest, not whether the page itself exists/is viewable, which this claim narrowly and correctly asserts; see CLM-053 for the paywall-mechanism claim and the MV-pricing-001 finding for the feature-honesty gap.)

claims.claim: Privacy policy and terms pages contain honest, accurate legal copy as part of a "legal-page honesty" fix -- not placeholder/lorem-ipsum content
claims.method: Visit /privacy-policy and /terms, read the content for genuineness (no placeholder/lorem text; plausible, specific data-handling claims)
claims.adjudicationNote: Privacy Policy: content genuine, no lorem/placeholder text, but was silent on Australian privacy law/jurisdiction despite the product being AU-market/GST-charging (MV-privacy-policy-002, VERIFIED-CLOSED, fixed). Terms: 3 defects found and fixed -- unfilled bracket placeholders in the pricing/ABN and refund-policy sections (MV-terms-002), a promised-but-nonexistent 'in-app support channel' contact mechanism (MV-terms-003), and an internally-inconsistent USD/Delaware liability-and-governing-law clause bolted onto an otherwise AUD/GST/ABN document (MV-terms-004). All fixed at 72d9f3f, qa-adversary-reverified fresh on prod.

claims.claim: Historical Seek-sourced job rows are retained in the database but hidden from every user-facing list (not shown in the job-discovery UI or API responses)
claims.method: GET /jobs and inspect the response for any Seek-origin source field; confirm none appear despite DB retention
claims.adjudicationNote: Hidden-from-user-facing-list half CONFIRMED: every GET /jobs probe across this run shows 0 Seek-origin rows in any user-facing response. The 'retained in the database' half is UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI -- no direct DB inspection for historical Seek rows was performed in this run.

claims.claim: The admin panel includes a signup toggle (enable/disable public signup) and an append-only audit log whose entries cannot be edited or deleted
claims.method: Requires admin access: toggle the signup setting and confirm signup is blocked/allowed accordingly; attempt to modify an audit-log entry via the API and confirm rejection or absence of any such endpoint
claims.adjudicationNote: Immutability half CONFIRMED live: DELETE /admin/audit-log -> 405 Method Not Allowed; DELETE/PATCH /admin/audit-log/{id} -> 404 (no id-scoped mutate route exists at all), reproduced twice. Signup-toggle functional-effect half (does flipping signupEnabled actually gate signup) is UNVERIFIABLE-FROM-UI/HUMAN-GATED -- requires admin access to flip it, which is absent in production.

claims.claim: The admin panel provides a data export/delete capability for user data
endpoints: (TBD - not explicitly listed in SCREEN-MATRIX's admin endpoint inventory; confirm exact path via admin UI network trace)
claims.method: Requires admin access: locate the data export/delete control in the admin UI and exercise it
claims.adjudicationNote: MV-admin-users-002 (VERIFIED-CLOSED, owner note: 'NO CODE CHANGE (claim overreach; 404/405 honest, no export/delete feature exists); reviewer-confirmed'): GET .../admin/users/{id}/export -> 404, DELETE /admin/users/{id} -> 405, reproduced twice; full source review of admin.py (all 10 admin operations) and main.py (every mounted router) confirms no export/GDPR/delete-user endpoint exists anywhere in the deployed API. The admin-root tester's own read was 'UNSURE, leaning REFUTED' pending a definitive check, since resolved definitively by MV-admin-users-002.

adjudication_pass.method: Harvested this-run TESTING-OUTCOME-REPORT.md claim-verdicts sections (28 screens), CROSS-CUTTING-NOTES.md, adversarial/*.json, MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json findings (154 total, 27 with direct claimRefs), MANUAL-VERIFICATION-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN.md, EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md; supplemented with fresh probes (curl against prod, systemctl, git, grep, targeted source-code reads) for claims lacking tester coverage.
